item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: PickPik
License: CC0
Title: gray whale on clouds at daytime
Author: N/A
Release year: N/A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If not part of the diet, you just, stay there.

Agent Byrne was basically stuck inside there for years and couldn't do anything about his sorry lot, knowing the second he's expelled out of the whale anomaly, whenever that is, that's it, he's dead.

Makes you wonder what the whale has eat and never released…

For example: bodies of Vikings recovered, DNA ancestors of William the Conqueror - died over a 1,000 years ago, of natural causes…..

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

After getting a chance to talk to Roget about this whale of an SCP, it was brought to my attention that both SCP-1608 and SCP-1712 share the use of MTF Kappa-11 (Red Barons), and there for have a link via containment methods. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Members of MTF-Kappa-11 "Red Barons" are to track and monitor SCP-1608's current location and report its status to Area-78. Airborne personnel are to maintain a distance of 150 meters away from SCP-1608's estimated location, to prevent their crafts from being damaged during containment events. Seaborne personnel are to report any manifestation events they observe. If containment is breached, members of MTF-Kappa-11 are to follow its hypothesized location until it manifests, then release food for it in a trail leading back to Area-78.

To this:

Members of MTF-Kappa-11 "Red Barons" are to track and monitor SCP-1608's current location and report its status to Area-78. Airborne personnel are to maintain a distance of 150 meters away from SCP-1608's estimated location, to prevent their crafts from being damaged during containment events. Seaborne personnel are to report any manifestation events they observe. If containment is breached, members of MTF-Kappa-11 are to follow its hypothesized location until it manifests, then release food for it in a trail leading back to Area-78.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reminds me of the temporal bubble anomaly in S.T.A.L.K.E.R., and that thing is creepy.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm also struggling with this tidbit. This one as well:

Flag originating from a Spanish vessel dating from at least 1776 was expelled from SCP-1608. Appeared to be drenched in SCP-1608's blood, however no damage was noted on SCP-1608.

How do they know it's its blood? Is the blood also intangible? If they learned this through DNA or something, how exactly? I supposed that, since it is intangible most of the time, it wouldn't be possible unless they either made good use of the 20 seconds where it is manifested, or if they somehow got some of its DNA back from previous expelled objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why do i get the feeling that this was sort of inspired by the whale in hitchikers guide to the galaxy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but:

Expelled materials appearing to belong with a British whaling ship, including a watch tower, circa 1898. The remains of an unidentified human was found preserved with the watch tower, and the subject appeared to have expired upon exiting SCP-1608.

And:

The remains of a Foundation aircraft and Agent Byrne were released. Autopsy showed Agent Byrne was killed upon exiting SCP-1608. No other abnormalities were noted with the body or aircraft.

It just feels like I'm not getting something. Why do they expire when they leave SCP-1608 as opposed to while they're inside it (like getting digested)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a flying Moby Dick. How better can this be?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The phrasing in the description is rather muddled. I had to back-track more than once before I understood clearly what was going on. For example, "SCP-1608 is intangible…" is misleading. It's usually intangible, manifesting briefly and irregularly.

Aside from that and some other phrasing I think could use an overhaul, the base idea just doesn't grab me. It's a cloud whale. That concept could be made cool, but as it is I am left un-hooked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm only mildly ashamed to admit I lol'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mass mass mass mass mass mass. Jesus, can you try to be a bit less spammy with that?

It is hypothesized that SCP-1608 manifests to breathe and excrete waste

Something seems to be missing here. Oh yeah…

When feeding, SCP-1608 will…re-manifest

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture is it coming out of invisibility, sort of in the in-betweeny stage

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure why it's impossible to track when it's clearly visible…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It could use some touch ups but you're already working on those I see. I like the concept. Thar be the great white one…

So does the whale digest them, or just kind of remove them from space-time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yu-Kaing

I believe that's spelled "Yu-Kiang".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but I think the writing needs some touchup. I was thrown a bit by the usage of 'intangible' I think, specifically with regards to the stomach.

Also, I find it highly bizarre that this skip was posted on the same day I saw these images on tumblr: 1 2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's just a whale in the sky

Whale in the sky keeps on turnin'
Don't know where I'll be tomorrow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. I think you rely too much on the fact that it's a skywhale that poops out things from that past. Like, that's all it does. It's just a whale in the sky. What's supposed to interest me about this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. I mean it's a sky whale that poops things out a while later but is that it or am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the whale just goes to nearest food, so by releasing birds we keep it floating around a limited, excluded area.
The procedures aren't quite kosher, but they aren't quite awful enough to make me downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good, but the containment procedures make no sense to me.

Still, have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahhhh, I see. I figured it was something like that, but I'm glad that was cleared up in any case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The report going with that dates shows it was observed by a whaling boat, not the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no canon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this because the concept seems pretty simple, and there is really no explaination at all. It's just an invisible floating whale…

One minor thing. Aren't 'Areas' designated for Keter class objects and 'Sites' designated for Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sky whale! :D

Fun idea with lots of great little touches to back it up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this is certainly…. original. Anyway, I like it. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this up and posted it over lunch, I'll fix any errors when I get home.

I've made some edits, hopefully it helps

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1608 was first observed by the Foundation on 8/11/1929

??/??/1928

Was this intentional, and I'm just not understanding it?

Anyway, this is grand! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Redaction in the Containment Procedures. The mind quails.

And I'm not drawn in enough by anything else in it to really earn it back. Airborne ghost-whale with a vendetta against whaling vessels. Seems… well it doesn't seem like much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a couple of them.
Like , you know, capitalized second part of a linnaean species name

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting concept, there are details lacking in the containment and a few typos that make my brain itch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, have a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one bothered by the grammatical errors and nonsensical measurements?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Agent Pinocchio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The detail about the Spanish boat is what really… hooked me.
